title: gayophytum helleri subsp. erosulatum
intro: Gayophytum helleri subsp. erosulatum, also known as a type of evening primrose, is a rare, flowering plant native to the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The plant's extracts have been reported to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial activity. Herbal preparations of Gayophytum helleri subsp. erosulatum include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to support skin health and address various health issues.
